title: Gemini, Gophers, and Fingers. Oh My Alternative Internets Beyond HTTPS

processed.summary:
* Explores the **architectural minimalism** of alternative protocols like Gemini, Gopher, and Finger as a reaction to the complexity and surveillance of the modern HTTPS-based web.
* Details the **Gemini protocol**, which utilizes a mandatory TLS layer for security while enforcing a strict, non-extensible text format (Gemtext) to prevent tracking and browser bloat.
* Analyzes the potential for **low-bandwidth networking** and decentralized content delivery systems that bypass central gatekeepers and heavy JavaScript execution.
key_results: Gemini provides a 'middle ground' between the extreme simplicity of Gopher and the overhead of modern HTTPS.
Gemtext format ensures content remains human-readable and easily parseable by minimalist clients.
Alternative protocols inherently resist tracking mechanisms like cookies and script-based fingerprinting.
The resurgence of the 'Small Web' encourages high-signal, text-only community interaction.
Simpler protocol stacks reduce the attack surface and hardware requirements for client applications.
processed.one_sentence_takeaway: Brennan Day advocates for alternative internet protocols like Gemini to regain digital autonomy and architectural simplicity beyond HTTPS.

title: How the ZX80 Works (2019)

processed.summary:

- A primary innovation is the **software-driven video generation**, where the processor is directly responsible for timing the television signal output.
- The system features a highly constrained **1K RAM** and **4K ROM**, necessitating extreme efficiency in both hardware layout and firmware design.
key_results: Achieved sub-£100 pricing through the use of standard off-the-shelf components.
Implemented a 'slow' mode where the CPU alternates between computation and video signal generation.
Utilized hardware-level memory mapping tricks to share data lines for video generation.
Reduced the motherboard footprint by simplifying the bus interface compared to contemporary machines.
Established the foundation for the ZX81 and ZX Spectrum via its minimalist design philosophy.

title: Go: Support for Generic Methods

The architectural impact involves significant changes to **interface satisfaction** and how the compiler handles dynamic dispatch for methods with independent type parameters.
key_results: Introduction of method-level type parameters for increased API flexibility.
Resolution of the 'generic method' limitation present since Go 1.18.

title: Ninth Circuit Panel Goes Out of Its Way to Question Section 230–DOE vs. Meta

processed.summary:

- The legal debate centers on whether **algorithmic recommendations** and platform design features qualify as protected publishing activity or as separate, non-immunized conduct.
- This judicial shift could impose new **legal liabilities** on providers of Generative AI and LLM agents who curate or amplify third-party content.
- The ruling highlights an increasing trend of courts questioning the **legacy liability frameworks** that have historically shielded tech platforms from litigation.

Potential distinction between neutral hosting and active content design.

title: Why is Vivado 2026.1 dropping Linux support for free tier?

processed.summary:
- AMD is updating its **Vivado Design Suite 2026.1** licensing model to remove Linux support from the **Standard Edition**, which is the platform's free tier.
- This policy shift forces hobbyists and academic researchers to use **Windows-only environments** or purchase expensive professional licenses to maintain Linux-based workflows.
- The change significantly impacts **CI/CD pipelines** and automated hardware-accelerated build systems that rely on Linux-based containerization and server infrastructure.
- The hardware engineering community expresses concern that this move will stifle **open-source hardware development** and push users toward alternative FPGA toolchains.
key_results: Vivado 2026.1 Standard Edition will no longer support Linux distributions.
Linux compatibility for the free tier is being phased out starting with the 2026.1 release cycle.
Users requiring Linux for FPGA development must upgrade to paid 'Enterprise' or 'Pro' licenses.
Academic and hobbyist developers on Linux must either remain on version 2025.x or migrate to Windows.
The move has sparked a surge of interest in alternative open-source FPGA tools like Yosys.
processed.one_sentence_takeaway: AMD restricts Vivado 2026.1 Linux support to paid tiers, forcing free-tier hardware developers into Windows-based development environments.

title: Scammers are abusing an internal Microsoft account to send spam links

- The attack highlights a significant vulnerability in **identity management** and credential security for internal automated messaging accounts.

title: JWT is a scam and your app doesn't need it

processed.summary:
• Challenges the common industry practice of using **JWT (JSON Web Tokens)** for session management, arguing that it introduces unnecessary complexity and security risks compared to traditional cookies.
• Critiques the **statelessness** argument, noting that any requirement for token revocation (e.g., logout or account banning) forces a stateful database check, which negates the primary architectural benefit of JWTs.
• Recommends the use of **opaque session tokens** stored in HttpOnly, Secure cookies to prevent XSS-based token theft and simplify server-side authentication logic.
key_results: JWTs are frequently used for session management where stateful cookies would be more secure.
True statelessness is rarely achieved because session revocation requires a central blacklist or database.
Storing JWTs in LocalStorage exposes applications to Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks.
Opaque tokens minimize the attack surface by keeping session data strictly server-side.
JWTs should be restricted to specific use cases like short-lived tokens for cross-service communication.
processed.one_sentence_takeaway: Dusan Malusev argues that developers should replace JWTs with opaque session cookies to improve security and reduce architectural complexity.

Automated content generation often bypasses basic quality assurance layers.
High-volume AI content strategies are frequently hindered by edge-case pronunciation errors.
